metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1631
metadata.comment: Rationale: Fig 5 shows that upon Septin 11 knockdown the number of dendritic protrusions is decreased in hippocampal neurons while their length is increased (Fig5B). This suggests a defect in mature spine formation. That this morphological difference also results in less synapses is shown for GABA, Fig5D shows that the number of postsynaptic GABA receptor clusters is decreased upon Septin 11 knock down, and also the overlap between GAD (presysnapse) and gamma2 (postsynapse), indicating that less synapses are formed. This is fully restored by reexpressing Septin 11, and not found in the shRNA with mutations that should not bind to Septin 11 message. These results are further verified by overexpression of a dominant negative Septin 11 that is linked to mCherry. The untagged Septin 11 overexpression has no effect (fig6A-E).. Experimental description: M&M: "Two shRNAs (sh1 and sh2) targeting the coding sequence of the rat septin 11.."

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
